{
  "decision": "fail",

  "percent_complete": 0,

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Untwisted pairs at termination exceed the maximum allowable limit of 0.5 inches (13 mm) for Category 5e/6/6A cabling."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Re-terminate the twisted-pair cabling ensuring that the pair twists are maintained up to the point of termination, not exceeding 0.5 inches of untwisted length."
  ],
  "rationale": "Per TIA-568 standards, maintaining pair twist up to the point of termination is critical to minimize near-end crosstalk (NEXT). For Category 5e and higher, the maximum allowable untwisted length at the termination point is 0.5 inches (13 mm). Exceeding this limit degrades signal integrity and will cause the link to fail certification testing.",
  "references": [
    "ANSI/TIA-568-C.2",
    "NEC Article 800"
  ]
}
